title: trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum
intro: Trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum, commonly known as Chinese milk vine, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is known to have various health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, likely due to its therapeutic actions such as vasodilation and antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Chinese milk vine include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic effects of Trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum, but initial findings suggest its potential as a natural remedy for various health conditions.

parts: Trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and stems, which have been used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as alkaloids like tetanocarpine. The roots of the plant are rich in iridoid glycosides, including tetanocarpine and other compounds like loganin and secologanin. The stems of Trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum also contain iridoid glycosides, such as loganin and secologanin, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

side_effects: Trachelospermum cathayanum var. tetanocarpum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems.
